description: Hollow revolved vase/bottle shape, open at top, centered at origin, defined by a piecewise-linear profile revolved 360° around Z axis, then shelled to 2mm wall thickness
components: Revolved solid body from piecewise-linear XZ profile
Shell operation removing top face to create hollow interior with 2mm wall
notes: Profile vertices in order (radius_mm, z_mm): (25,0), (25,5), (28,25), (22,45), (10,55), (10,65). Revolve this closed profile 360° around Z axis using CadQuery revolve or equivalent. After revolving the solid, apply a shell operation with 2mm thickness, specifying the top face (at Z=65, the neck opening circle of radius 10mm) as the face to remove/open. The shell should offset all remaining faces inward by 2mm, creating a hollow interior. Ensure the bottom is closed (not open). The part is centered in XY at origin (X=0, Y=0). The bounding box in X and Y is ±28mm (belly radius), giving 56mm total in both X and Y. Z runs from 0 to 65mm. The neck opening inner diameter after shelling will be 20mm outer minus 4mm (2×wall) = 16mm inner, but the open top face remains open. Coder should use CadQuery: build a 2D wire/face from the profile points, revolve, then shell with top face excluded.
